message:
feat(workflows/engine): tick 188 resume_cx + find_incomplete_cx thread cx into storage (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

The ft-xbnl0.2.2 Workflow-trait cx-first helpers on WorkflowEngine
(resume_cx, find_incomplete_cx) used the lightweight "entry checkpoint
→ delegate to legacy → exit checkpoint" pattern. That pattern adds
cancellation seams at the boundary but leaves the inner storage reads
running under ambient cx — a concrete orphan-cx hole whenever a caller
cancels mid-body.

This tick closes both holes by routing through the cx-first storage
siblings (already present from the 140/140 sibling sweep, tick 149):

- resume_cx now inlines its body with cx-threaded storage calls:
    storage.get_workflow_with_cx(cx, execution_id)
    storage.get_step_logs_with_cx(cx, execution_id)
  rather than delegating to resume(). Reconciliation logic
  (resolve_resume_step) and WorkflowExecution construction unchanged.

- find_incomplete_cx now routes to find_incomplete_workflows_with_cx
  directly instead of calling the ambient-cx find_incomplete.

Legacy `resume` and `find_incomplete` are preserved unchanged so
non-asupersync builds and callers still passing ambient cx see no
behavior change.

Verification: 1168 / 1168 workflows:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass
(CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Orphan-cx progress on the workflows subsystem:
- run_workflow_inner strangler-fig complete (ticks 178-187, 56+ seams)
- WorkflowEngine cx-first helpers now cx-threaded end-to-end (this)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
